name: next-cache-components
description: Next.js 16 Cache Components - PPR, use cache directive, cacheLife, cacheTag, updateTag
capabilities: Explain Cache Components and Partial Prerendering architecture
Provide code examples for 'use cache' directive at file, component, and function levels
Show cache invalidation patterns using cacheTag, updateTag, and revalidateTag
Demonstrate cache profiles and custom lifetime configuration
Help migrate from older Next.js caching APIs to Cache Components
author: vercel-labs
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing code examples for Next.js 16 caching features. All 82 static findings are false positives: backticks in code blocks flagged as shell commands, fetch examples in documentation, and crypto APIs in examples. No executable code, network calls, or security risks present.
valueStatement: Next.js applications often fetch data repeatedly, causing slow page loads. Cache Components enable Partial Prerendering and intelligent caching strategies to serve cached content instantly while streaming dynamic parts.
userTitle: Optimize Next.js Performance with Cache Components
limitations: Cannot access cookies, headers, or searchParams inside 'use cache' blocks
Edge runtime is not supported - requires Node.js server runtime
Static export mode (next export) is not supported
Non-deterministic values like Math.random() execute once at build time

useCases.title: Optimize Dashboard Pages
useCases.description: Create dashboard pages with static shell, cached stats, and streaming notifications using Suspense boundaries.

useCases.title: Implement CMS Caching Strategy
useCases.description: Cache blog posts and product pages with intelligent invalidation when content changes via cacheTag.

useCases.title: Migrate from Legacy Caching
useCases.description: Update older Next.js projects using experimental.ppr or unstable_cache to modern Cache Components.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Cache Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I enable Cache Components in Next.js 16? Show me the configuration and basic usage pattern.

promptTemplates.title: Cache Invalidation
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to invalidate my product cache when inventory changes. Show me how to use cacheTag and updateTag for immediate invalidation.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Cache Lifetime
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set custom cache lifetime with cacheLife()? Show me both built-in profiles and inline configuration for stale-while-revalidate behavior.

promptTemplates.title: Migration Patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm migrating from experimental.ppr and dynamic = 'force-static'. What is the equivalent Cache Components pattern for revalidate = 3600?

bestPractices: Use 'use cache' at the function level for fine-grained control over what gets cached
Apply cacheTag() to enable targeted invalidation when data sources update
Combine cached content with Suspense boundaries to maintain fast initial page loads
Test cache behavior with different profiles (minutes, hours, days) before deploying
antiPatterns: Accessing cookies(), headers(), or searchParams directly inside 'use cache' blocks - pass them as arguments instead
Caching highly dynamic user-specific data that changes on every request
Using Math.random() or Date.now() inside cached functions - they execute once at build time
Forgetting to add cacheTag() before implementation - makes invalidation impossible

faq.question: What is the difference between updateTag() and revalidateTag()?
faq.answer: updateTag() invalidates immediately within the same request, so subsequent reads see fresh data. revalidateTag() triggers background revalidation - the next request sees stale data while revalidating in the background.

faq.question: Can I use 'use cache' with Edge Runtime?
faq.answer: No. Cache Components require the Node.js server runtime. Edge runtime does not support the caching infrastructure needed for 'use cache' directive.

faq.question: How do I handle user-specific data with Cache Components?
faq.answer: Pass user-specific data as function arguments. The arguments become part of the cache key, so each user gets their own cached version. Do not access cookies() or headers() inside 'use cache'.

faq.question: What happens to cached data when I deploy a new version?
faq.answer: Cache is automatically invalidated by the build ID. Deploying a new version changes the build ID, so all cached content is treated as fresh and repopulated on first access.

faq.question: How do I debug cache behavior in development?
faq.answer: Check the Next.js DevTools cache panel. You can also add console.log statements inside 'use cache' blocks - they only execute when the cache is being populated, not on cache hits.

faq.question: What is the default cache behavior without any configuration?
faq.answer: Without 'use cache', content is either static (synchronous code) or dynamic (suspended). With 'use cache' alone, it uses the default profile: 5 minutes stale, 15 minutes revalidation.
